articles.title: Cloud development platform Vercel was hacked
articles.summary: Vercel, a major web development platform, was hacked by ShinyHunters who are selling stolen data including employee names and emails. The company confirmed the security incident affected a limited subset of users.

articles.title: The RAM shortage could last years
articles.summary: RAM shortage expected to persist until 2027-2030 as suppliers can only meet 60% of demand despite increased DRAM production. Major chipmakers Samsung, SK Hynix, and Micron are ramping up efforts to address the deficit.

articles.title: OpenAI’s former Sora boss is leaving
articles.summary: Bill Peebles, leader of OpenAI's Sora video generation team, is departing the company after OpenAI discontinued the project. The departure reflects OpenAI's strategic shift to focus on coding and enterprise solutions.

articles.title: OpenAI’s existential questions
articles.summary: OpenAI's recent acquisitions are examined to determine if they address two major existential challenges facing the company.

articles.title: The 12-month window
articles.summary: AI startups currently thrive in niches untouched by foundation models, but this advantage is temporary as these models expand into more categories.

articles.title: Palantir posts mini-manifesto denouncing inclusivity and ‘regressive’ cultures
articles.summary: Palantir released a statement criticizing inclusivity and 'regressive' cultures while defending Western values. The company faces scrutiny for its ICE work and ideological positioning.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native infrastructure challenging AWS and Google Cloud by addressing legacy platform limitations.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code charges $20-$200/month for AI coding, prompting backlash. Goose, a free open-source alternative by Block, offers identical functionality locally without subscription fees.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B after a viral billboard hiring campaign using AI tokens and coding challenges. The unconventional stunt successfully recruited top engineering talent to compete with tech giants' recruitment efforts.

articles.title: US-sanctioned currency exchange says $15 million heist done by "unfriendly states"
articles.summary: Grinex, a US-sanctioned cryptocurrency exchange, halted operations after a $15 million heist attributed to state-sponsored hackers. Researchers confirmed the theft targeted Russian users through sophisticated attacks.

articles.title: Recent advances push Big Tech closer to the Q-Day danger zone
articles.summary: The 2010 Flame malware exploited MD5 cryptography to forge Microsoft certificates and distribute malicious updates. This incident highlights growing cybersecurity vulnerabilities as quantum computing advances threaten current encryption methods.

articles.title: “Negative” views of Broadcom driving thousands of VMware migrations, rival says
articles.summary: Broadcom's VMware takeover has driven customer dissatisfaction, allowing rival Nutanix to attract thousands of VMware customers. Nutanix CEO claims the company now has about 30,000 customers, many migrated from VMware.

articles.title: Pie Day 2026
articles.summary: Ellie organized a Pi Day 2026 event at MIT's Food Institute, orchestrating the baking of 30 pies. The posts detail her celebration and behind-the-scenes process.

articles.title: The Download: bad news for inner Neanderthals, and AI warfare’s human illusion
articles.summary: French geneticists challenge the popular theory that modern humans carry Neanderthal DNA from ancient interbreeding, proposing population structure as an alternative explanation for genetic similarities.

articles.title: The case for fixing everything
articles.summary: Stewart Brand's new book 'Maintenance: Of Everything, Part One' argues that upkeep and repair of tools and systems are civilizationally important and a radical act deserving honor for maintainers.

articles.title: Building a Fast Multilingual OCR Model with Synthetic Data
articles.summary: Researchers developed a fast multilingual optical character recognition model using synthetic data generation. The approach enables efficient text recognition across multiple languages without extensive labeled datasets.

articles.title: Ecom-RLVE: Adaptive Verifiable Environments for E-Commerce Conversational Agents
articles.summary: Ecom-RLVE introduces adaptive verifiable environments for training e-commerce conversational agents using reinforcement learning, improving agent performance in realistic shopping scenarios.

articles.title: The PR you would have opened yourself
articles.summary: Article discusses how AI systems can autonomously generate pull requests and code contributions, potentially automating development work that developers would manually create themselves.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI technology and upcoming developments in the field. The conversation explores what innovations and challenges lie ahead for artificial intelligence.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to boost productivity and creativity in content creation and marketing tasks.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft releases its 'farm of the future' toolkit as open source, enabling broader access to agricultural AI technology for sustainable farming innovations.

articles.title: 7 ways to travel smarter this summer, with help from Google
articles.summary: Google offers latest tools to help travelers plan trips, find deals, and explore destinations this summer with smarter travel solutions.

articles.title: A new way to explore the web with AI Mode in Chrome
articles.summary: Chrome's new AI Mode upgrades transform web interaction. The feature enables users to explore and engage with web content using artificial intelligence capabilities.

articles.title: New ways to create personalized images in the Gemini app
articles.summary: Nano Banana 2 now generates personalized images using your Google Photos and personal context, creating visuals that reflect your unique life and experiences.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS: the next generation of expressive AI speech
articles.summary: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS introduces granular audio tags enabling precise control over AI-generated speech for expressive audio generation.

articles.title: Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6: Powering real-world robotics tasks through enhanced embodied reasoning
articles.summary: Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 enhances spatial reasoning and multi-view understanding to improve autonomous robots' real-world task performance through advanced embodied reasoning capabilities.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google releases Gemma 4, its most capable open-source models yet, designed specifically for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and higher per-token prices but improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family featuring mixture-of-experts architecture.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia launched DLSS 5, a generative AI graphics technology that enhances video game photorealism in real-time up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting conditions.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 discusses major AI news including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR pricing, and new interactive features from ChatGPT and Anthropic for visualizations and charts.

articles.title: OpenAI Agents SDK improves governance with sandbox execution
articles.summary: OpenAI introduces sandbox execution in its Agents SDK, enabling enterprise governance teams to deploy automated workflows with controlled risk and improved visibility into operations.

articles.title: Cadence expands AI and robotic partnerships with Nvidia, Google Cloud
articles.summary: Cadence Design Systems expanded AI partnerships with Nvidia and Google Cloud, integrating simulation tools with Nvidia's CUDA-X and Omniverse for robotic systems and semiconductor design.

articles.title: Commvault launches a ‘Ctrl-Z’ for cloud AI workloads
articles.summary: Commvault launched AI Protect, an undo feature for cloud AI workloads that monitors and rolls back autonomous AI agent actions across AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud to address governance risks.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and determine when failures occur during collaborative problem-solving.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance developed Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses localization, self-positioning, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data and updating their own weights through reinforcement learning.

articles.title: The paradox of LLM self-distillation: Faster reasoning, weaker generalization
articles.summary: Self-distilling LLMs for faster, concise answers reduces reasoning time but weakens generalization and problem-solving on complex tasks. The optimization creates a tradeoff between speed and capability.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As large language models become commoditized, the sophisticated engineering systems built around them are emerging as the true competitive advantage for AI companies.

articles.title: TopDawg vs Zendrop for US Dropshipping – Which Platform Is Better in 2026?
articles.summary: TopDawg excels for US-focused merchants with US-based fulfillment and hands-on support, while Zendrop suits sellers needing global reach and self-serve features.

articles.title: Moonshot AI and Tsinghua Researchers Propose PrfaaS: A Cross-Datacenter KVCache Architecture that Rethinks How LLMs are Served at Scale
articles.summary: Moonshot AI and Tsinghua researchers propose PrfaaS, a cross-datacenter architecture that separates LLM prefill and decode operations, achieving 54% higher throughput than traditional single-datacenter approaches using commodity networks.

articles.title: Meet OpenMythos: An Open-Source PyTorch Reconstruction of Claude Mythos Where 770M Parameters Match a 1.3B Transformer
articles.summary: OpenMythos is an open-source PyTorch reconstruction theorizing Claude Mythos architecture, where 770M parameters match 1.3B transformer performance. Researcher Kye Gomez created this hypothesis-based implementation grounded in peer-reviewed research, not leaked models.

articles.title: How TabPFN Leverages In-Context Learning to Achieve Superior Accuracy on Tabular Datasets Compared to Random Forest and CatBoost
articles.summary: TabPFN challenges traditional tree-based models like Random Forest and CatBoost by using in-context learning on tabular data, avoiding dataset-specific training and achieving superior accuracy.

articles.title: I hid 4 Bluetooth trackers (including AirTags) to test their reliability - here's how Android rivals compared
articles.summary: A test compared AirTags with Android Bluetooth trackers to evaluate their reliability in finding lost items. AirTags, released in 2021, remain competitive against rival tracking solutions.

articles.title: I stopped using my iPhone's hotspot after testing this 5G router - and that won't change
articles.summary: The Acer Connect M6E 5G router provides fast connectivity without draining phone battery, offering a better alternative to using iPhone's hotspot feature.

articles.title: How I share audio from my Android phone to multiple earbuds (and why it's a big deal)
articles.summary: Android's audio sharing feature enables two people to listen simultaneously on separate earbuds from one device, provided both devices support the technology.

articles.title: The Complete Guide to Inference Caching in LLMs
articles.summary: Inference caching optimizes LLM API calls by reducing costs and latency at scale through intelligent caching mechanisms.

articles.title: Python Decorators for Production Machine Learning Engineering
articles.summary: Article discusses using Python decorators in production machine learning engineering to improve code efficiency and functionality.

articles.title: 5 Techniques for Efficient Long-Context RAG
articles.summary: Article presents five techniques to improve efficiency in long-context Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems, enabling better handling of extended documents and contexts.

articles.title: Proxy-Pointer RAG: Structure Meets Scale at 100% Accuracy with Smarter Retrieval
articles.summary: Proxy-Pointer RAG introduces an open-source retrieval method achieving 100% accuracy with a 5-minute setup, combining structured data handling with scalable vector-based retrieval.

articles.title: Dreaming in Cubes
articles.summary: Researchers use VQ-VAE and Transformers to generate Minecraft worlds, combining vector quantization with deep learning for procedural world creation.

articles.title: KV Cache Is Eating Your VRAM. Here’s How Google Fixed It With TurboQuant.
articles.summary: Google's TurboQuant framework optimizes KV cache memory usage through multi-stage compression techniques like PolarQuant and QJL residuals, enabling larger context windows with minimal VRAM overhead.

articles.title: No Need for Space Gear — Capcom’s ‘PRAGMATA’ Joins GeForce NOW on Launch Day
articles.summary: Capcom's sci-fi action game PRAGMATA launches on GeForce NOW the same day as its worldwide release, allowing cloud streaming to any device. Five additional titles join the platform this week.

articles.title: Rethinking AI TCO: Why Cost per Token Is the Only Metric That Matters
articles.summary: Data centers have transformed into AI token factories, making cost per token the crucial metric for evaluating AI infrastructure TCO rather than traditional compute costs or FLOPS per dollar.

articles.title: New Adobe Premiere Color Grading Mode Accelerated on NVIDIA GPUs
articles.summary: Adobe announces a new Premiere Color Mode in beta at NAB Show 2026, accelerated by NVIDIA RTX GPUs to enhance video editing workflows for professional creators.

articles.title: Introducing granular cost attribution for Amazon Bedrock
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock now offers granular cost attribution for AI inference, automatically tracking costs by IAM principal and enabling detailed billing allocation across models without additional configuration.

articles.title: Optimize video semantic search intent with Amazon Nova Model Distillation on Amazon Bedrock
articles.summary: Amazon Nova Model Distillation on Bedrock optimizes video semantic search by balancing accuracy and latency. The approach uses intelligent intent routing to reduce search time from 2-4 seconds while maintaining strong performance.

articles.title: Power video semantic search with Amazon Nova Multimodal Embeddings
articles.summary: Amazon Nova Multimodal Embeddings enable video semantic search, allowing organizations to quickly find specific moments within videos for sports highlights, archived content, and news footage to enhance user experiences and monetization.

articles.title: Full-Stack Optimizations for Agentic Inference with NVIDIA Dynamo
articles.summary: NVIDIA Dynamo enables full-stack optimizations for agentic inference, allowing AI coding agents to generate production code at scale. Companies like Stripe and Ramp report significant productivity gains with agents creating thousands of pull requests weekly.

articles.title: Build a More Secure, Always-On Local AI Agent with OpenClaw and NVIDIA NemoClaw
articles.summary: OpenClaw and NVIDIA NemoClaw enable secure, always-on local AI agents that perform autonomous multi-step workflows beyond simple Q&A, including file reading and API calls.

articles.title: Accelerate Clean, Modular, Nuclear Reactor Design with AI Physics
articles.summary: AI is accelerating the development of small modular reactors (SMRs) and Generation IV nuclear designs to create safer, cleaner, and more economical nuclear energy solutions through physics-informed optimization.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: Research study explores how different generations perceive and respond to social media advertisements across various platforms, revealing generational differences in consumer attitudes toward ads.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to net zero emissions by 2030, developing technology to reduce its carbon footprint and sharing innovations openly.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: A person with a non-traditional background pioneered innovative XR technology, demonstrating how unconventional paths can lead to breakthrough innovations in extended reality development.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers are developing methods to identify and understand interactions at scale in Large Language Models, focusing on feature attribution and interpretability to make AI systems more transparent and trustworthy.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on information-driven design of imaging systems that uses encoders to map objects to images while accounting for noise corruption. An information estimator quantifies measurement quality using only noisy data and noise models.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new RL algorithm uses divide-and-conquer paradigm instead of temporal difference learning, offering better scalability for long-horizon tasks without TD learning's limitations.
